seoTitle: How to Add Images to a Microsoft Loop Page (2026)
metaDescription: Insert, resize, and caption images inside Microsoft Loop pages using slash commands, local uploads, or web links for cleaner visual documentation.
intro: Text-only Loop pages can be hard to scan when you're documenting mockups, screenshots, or visual references. This guide shows how to insert images into a Loop page, resize them to fit your layout, and add captions so your team understands the context at a glance.

steps.heading: Open your Loop page and enter edit mode
steps.body: Navigate to the workspace canvas where you want the image and place your cursor on an empty line.

steps.heading: Insert the image block
steps.body: Type the /image or /media slash command to open the media insertion container.

steps.heading: Upload a local file or paste a web link
steps.body: Either browse and drag a JPEG or PNG from your device directly onto the canvas, or paste a direct image URL to auto-render an externally hosted graphic.

steps.heading: Resize and align the image
steps.body: Click the inserted image to reveal the alignment handles, then drag the edges to scale the width so it fits your page layout.

steps.heading: Add a caption
steps.body: Click below the image to add a short descriptive caption so viewers understand what the image shows.

faq.question: Can I paste an image directly from my clipboard into Loop?
faq.answer: Loop supports dragging and dropping local image files, and pasting an image URL will auto-render the graphic, making both quick ways to get visuals onto a page.

faq.question: Will my images stay aligned on mobile and desktop?
faq.answer: Loop's image blocks are designed to scale with the page layout, though it's worth checking how a resized image looks across devices before sharing widely.

faq.question: Can I add alt text to images in Loop?
faq.answer: Yes, the caption field under an inserted image can double as descriptive context for accessibility and clarity.

faq.question: Why isn't my image showing up after I paste a link?
faq.answer: Make sure the URL points directly to an image file and is publicly accessible, since broken or permission-restricted links won't render as a preview.
